event.name: City Watch
event.rules: The City Watch is responding to citizen reports of particularly aggressive vagrants and brazen pickpockets outside the Settlement gates. Your Warrior is among those who are detained by the angry(and extremely corrupt) guards. One item of Treasure or piece of Equipment is confiscated as a "stolen" article (determined at random) and your Warrior is brutally flogged and must deduct 1D3 from their Starting Wounds permanently.

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Bandits
event.rules: Inanactofrevengeforthelossofoneof theirleaders,a group of vicious bandits emerge from the hills on horseback,raiding and looting the helpless peasants outside the Settlement.Your Warrior mustroll1D6 on the table below: 1 Your Warrior is soon targeted by the bandits who overpower them bysheernumbers.Theystealallof theirgoldand1D3itemsofTreasure (your choice) before the Settlement soldiers are able to drive them back into the hills. 2-3 Theattackcatcheseveryonebysurpriseand,despite the Warrior's best efforts to defend their belongings, the bandits make off with oneitem ofTreasure(your choice). 4-5 Your Warrior hides behind the makeshifttents,quietly slashing bandits astheyrideby.WhentheSettlement soldiers arrive,theyrewardyour Warrior with 1D6x25 gold for helping to defend the Settlement inhabitants. 6 Withadramaticleapoffathatchedroof,yourWarrior attacksthelargest,mostpowerfulbanditin thegroup, killing him outright.Theleaderlessattackersare easily routed by the Settlementsoldiers.TheSettlement leaders reward your Warrior with 1D6x50 gold for their role in the fight and they are allowed entry into the Settlement.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Argil's Magic Emporium
event.rules: Argil Fend le petter, a dishevelled travelling purveyor of salves and unguents,has setup shop here for the day.Your Warrior m ay purchase Argil's waresby following all of the normal rules of visiting a Traderin the Settle m ent. Cost ITEM Stock Description Buy 5 Heals 1D3 Wounds. 25g Liniment Tonic 6 50g Heals 1D6 Wounds. 100g Restores1pointtoany 6 Balm Statistic that has previously been reduced for anyreason. 150g Heals Warrior to full. 10 Home Brew 175g Gives your Warrior double Quicksilver Attacks for 1 Turn. When using any of Argil'sitemsroll1D6.Ifthe scoreis1 your Warriorloses1 Wound permanently and the item hasnoeffect. If the score is a 2 or3 your Warrior loses 1 Wound permanently but the item worksasstated.On a scoreof4,5 or 6theitem worksas stated with no side effects.

event.name: Beggars
event.rules: Your Warrior cannot hide their heroic stature and dozens of peasants soon surround them begging for money andfood. that half of allthe Provisions they were carrying are gone (roundeddown)and theyare1D6x20goldlighter! 3

event.name: Bard
event.rules: A colour fully dressed minstrel who is heading toward the t gates notices your Warrior. with a look of Settlement recognition 1 he excitedly approaches your Warrior, loudly strum ming his instrument and singing a ballad allabout your Warrior'sexploits.Roll1D6below: 1-2 While the lyrical poet relates all of the interesting exploits of your Warrior,a sm allcrowd gathers around, excitedly.Ayoungloutsuddenlylashesout,stabbing your Warriorwithapoisonousdagger!Hehappensto be the offspring of a criminal who your Warrior once broughttojustice.Theyoungscampisquicklysubdued by the other peasants and your hero is rushed to a wise man whomanagestoremovemuchof thevenom.Your Warrior's life is saved but must deduct 1 Wound from their Starting Wounds score permanently. 3-6 The bard's music focuses on all of your Warrior's good deedsandheroics!SoonyourWarriorismobbedby the the rest of the day giving blessings and allowing the wretched to kiss their boots and touch their sweat soaked handkerchief.

event.name: Hay Wagon
event.rules: Your Warrior sees a wagon filled with hay enter through the Settlement gates, presumably to feed the various animals inside.A second hay wagon canbe seen approaching from a distance and your Warrior has the crazy idea that they could possibly steal away inside the wagonin orderto get backinto theSettlement.IfyourWarriorchoosestodoso,roll1D6onthe tablebelow: 1 Injured!Asthe wagonreachestheSettlementGate,it is stopped by the guards who begin stabbing their swordsrandomlyinto thehaypile.Asoneof thesword thrusts penetrates your Warrior'sstomach,theyletout ablood-curdlingcry!Rollanother1D6andifthescoreis a1,yourWarriorexpiresonthespotandisremoved from the game.Onany other score they survive although they mustdeduct1 Wound permanently from their Starting Woundsscore.YourWarriorspendthe next 1D6 daysinjail,isfined 100 gold and is thrown back outof the Settlement. 2-3 Caught!AnobservantgateguardseesyourWarrior's foot sticking partiallyoutof thehay.Your Warrior is wrenched from the wagon and thrown into a jail cell for 1D3days.Theyarefined1o0goldbeforebeingthrown 4-5 Unnoticed!Thehaywagonpassesthroughthegates with your Warrior hidden in the hay and noone is the wiser!Afew momentsafteritstops,your Warrior hops outunobserved.YourWarriormaynowfollowallthe normal rules for staying in aSettlement. 6 Spotted!YourWarriorhidesin thehay wagonand enters the Settlementunnoticed.Whentheyfeelitis the wagon is parked in front of thejail!Standingnextto your Warrior is the captain of the watch who eyes them suspiciously."Notimefornapping!" he snorts."This hayneedstogetdeliveredto theanimalpens." The captain hands your Warrior 1D6x10gold coins and sends them on their way!Your W arrior m ay now follow all the normal rules for staying in a Settlement. 4

event.name: Outbreak
event.rules: There is an outbreak of Tyrant's Fever and your Warrior quickly becomes bed-ridden,their face becomes red and painfully swollen.They mustroll1D6atthebeginning ofeach dayonthe following table adding +1to the score each subsequent time (remembering the Rule of1and 6). 1 The fever has taken a turn for the worse and your Warrior can only moanin pain,unabletoleavetheirbed. Your Warriorsuffers-1to Strength or Toughness (your choice)forthenext Adventure.IftheirStrength or Toughnesseverreacheszero,theyaredead. 2-5 The fever runs high and your Warrior is bedridden in their makeshift hovel. Whilst laid up,your Warrior does 6 The fever breaks and your Warrior returns to normal by the end of the day.

event.name: Horse Thief
event.rules: While your Warrior is busy relieving himself in a m a ke shift latrine,ahorsethief makes off with theirmount.Ifyour Warrior has a horse, markit offyour Adventure Record Sheet along with anything that was attached it, such as a cart. If your Warriorhasnomounts,treatitasanUneventfulDayinstead.

event.name: Riot
event.rules: Stoked by the words of a few malcontents the lowly inhabitants who dwell outside of the Settlement have had enough of their impoverished situation. A small gathering has erupted into a dangerous riot pitting the peasantsagainstthelocalWatch.Roll 1D6onthetablebelow: 1 Your Warrior is arrested and charged with inciting ariot They(ortheircompanions) mustpay 1D6x200 goldin fines and bribes to clear up the matter.Iftheycannot pay they are hangedin the center of the Settlement in 1D6daysasawarningto theothervagabondsOutside theGates. Your Warrior is beaten terribly by the Watch and 2-3 thrownintojail.Theymustpay1D6x100gold.Ifthey cannotpaytheymustspendthenext1D6 days in jail 4-5 Joiningtherioters,your Warrior strides steadfastly into the Settlement where the angry mob demand sample sustenance,clean water and accessto medical treatment.TheSettlementofficials eventually agreeto acompromiseandeachpeasant,includingyour Warrior, isgiven 1D3 Provisions,1D3Bandages and1D6x5gold. 6 Organizingtherioters,your Warrior marches the pack of peasants into the Settlement demanding to meet with high officials.Theterrifiedauthorities quickly meet the demands of your Warrior and the peasants are given accesstofood,clean waterand medical facilities. ThepeasantsandyourWarriorareeachgiven1D6 Provisionsand1D6Bandages.YourWarrioralso receives1D6x50goldinpaymentasanegotiatorand for keeping the peasants from using violence to solve the matter. SE

event.name: The Black Tiger
event.rules: A masked criminal,the Black Tiger,sneaksinto your Warrior's makeshift camp in the middle of the night.Hestealsfrom the rich and gives to the poor.Ifyour Warrior has more than 1oo gold,the outlaw steals half. If your Warrior has less than 100 gold,theoutlaw does nothing.Ifyour Warrior has no gold atall, the Black Tiger slips 1oo gold pieces into your Warrior's purse. In any case,the outlaw leaves a piece of parchment with a tiger's paw print on your Warrior's chest which they findin the morning upon waking. Note: if the Bounty Hunter rolls this Event,im mediate ly roll up a new Outlaw following allthe normalrules. This Outlaw also has the Vanish 5+ Ability in addition to any others. (See the Bounty Hunter Revision by Littlemonk).

event.name: Bad Omen
event.rules: Your Warrior continually glimpses a black cat prowling about nearthem. Others in the vicinity also notice and swear it is a bad omen.Your Warrior is so perturbed by the situation that Adventure.

event.name: Wizard's Apprentice
event.rules: Late at night your Warrior witnesses a thin, frail man being assaulted by a group of hoodlums. Rushing to his aid your Warrior fends off the attackers whoscatterinto thenight.The man gasps for breath as blood pours from anopen woundon hishead.Bythelook of his clothes and belongings the young manappearstobeanapprenticingwizard.Fartooinjuredtobe healed by bandages,your Warrior can onlysave him ifthey give themanahealingpotion. Ifyour Warrior does so,theapprenticerepaysyour Warrior by casting a powerful spell that permanently increases one of 1 1D6 to see which Warrior's Characteristics. Roll your Characteristic is affected: Initiative 1 4 Weapon Skill Willpower 2 5 Strength 3 Luck Toughness 6 If your Warrior has no healing potion, the young apprentice expires...

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Curse
event.rules: into a patch of dead weeds and flowers adjacent to a crude dwelling. An old crone im mediate ly em erges yelling and screaming that your Warrior has destroyed her perfect lilies. She levels her finger at your Warrior and curses them malevolently before returning to her abode. Your Warrior must im mediatelydeduct1Luckpointpermanently!

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Animal Rampage
event.rules: attackingthepeasants.Your Warrior mustroll2D6.Thisisthe number of animals attacking them.Rollanother 1D6and add your W arrior's Battle-level,the score beingthe amount of wild animals your Warrior kills. If any animals remain, your Warrior injures their hand in theskirmishandsuffers-1 WeaponS kill for the next Adventure.

event.name: Stench
event.rules: The smell of urine and excrement is particularly overwhelming second time during their stay atthis Settlement,then they are so sickened by the smell that they suffer-1 Strength forthe next Adventure.

event.name: Famished
event.rules: Your Warrior must eat a provision or other food they are carrying to maintain their health and strength.Ifthey have no food,your Warriorsuffers-1Strengthin thenextAdventure.

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Collapse
event.rules: In the middle of the night a wall collapses upon your Warrior's m a ke shift cam p. Your Warrior's legis severely injured and they areunabletotravelfor1D3daysandmustremain outside the gates of theSettlement.In addition they suffer-1Movementfor the next Adventure. Whilstinjured,your Warrior does not have

event.name: Spirit
event.rules: The spirit of a druid appears in front of your Warrior late at night and accuses them of destroying sacred foliage during their recent journey to the Settlement.He demands that your Warrior make amends by planting atree or bushin a shady grove.Your Warrior suffers-1 Luck until they do so.Ittakes 1D3 days to make amends and your Warrior must rollfor 5

event.name: Noble
event.rules: An influential noble and his entourage are galloping through toward the Settlement gates and soon a small crowd has blocked their way to beg for food and coin. The noble inexplicably spots your Warrior amongst the crowd and points a fingeratthem.Roll1D6 one the following table: 1 The noble is extremely irritated that the crowdhas stoppedhim.Itisobviousto him thatyour Warrior, beingstoutandstrongcomparedto thefrailbeggars,is theoneincharge.Hedirectshisguardstosurround your Warrior aggressively and they drag them in front of thenobleforaseverebeating.Your Warrior must deduct1from their Starting Wounds score permanently. 2 The greedy noble eyes one of your Warrior's it emf Treasure(determinedatrandom)andsendshislackeys toobtainit.Uponpain ofdeath,your Warrior reluctantly hands over the item. 3 The noble is suspicious ofyour Warrior and calls for the Watch to apprehend and interrogatethem.For1D3 daysyour Warrior is grilled and beaten until eventually being fined 1D6x10 goldforloitering(your Warrior m ustsell item sorequipmentifnecessary).Theyare 4 Among all of the wretched beggars the compassionate noblehaschosenyouastherecipientof1D6x10gold. 5 Thenoble keenly observes that your Warrior may be of someuseforaparticularlyimportanttask.Heoffersto hire them for the day,payingthem 1D6x20gold to watch hishorse while in the Settlement.In addition, your Warrior m ay visit any Location not previously visited during this stay in the Settlement and must pay LivingExpensesasnormal.YourWarriorreturns 6 The noble calls out your name and beckons you over. Familiar with your recent heroic undertakings he wishes todonate1D6x100goldtoyourcause.IfyourWarrior wasnotallowedinto theSettlementforanyreason,he berates the watchcom m anderand Settlement officials whoim mediate ly apologize to your Warrior for any misunderstandingsandallowthemre-entry.Youmay treat this as a new visit to the Settlement and your Warrior m ay visit Traders and Special Locations even they had done so previously in this Settlement.

event.name: Pests
event.rules: Mischievous wild animals sneak into your Warrior's camp and pilfer some of your Warrior'spossessions.Roll1D6to see what they have taken: 1 Anitem ofTreasure(determined atrandom). 2 A Potion(determined atrandom). 3 ARope. 4 ABandage. 5 A Provision. Nothing. 6 Ifthe Warrior has such asitem they mustdiscardit.Ifthey don't have it,they does nothing.In any case,rollagain.Ifyou roll lower than your first score to see whattheanimalstole,the animal also stole the second item too,and soon.Assoon as you roll the same number again orhigher,stoprolling. 6

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Pet Dog
event.rules: Your Warrior is adopted byasmalldog.Itfollowsthem around and then emerging after the adventure is completed to shower itsnew master withadoration.Rollonthe Pet Dog Event Table (page 24) to see exactly which type of canine has attached itselftoyou. Your Warrior can killthe dogif you want,butit would be a crueland heartless thing to do andit will cost them 1,ooo gold to pay for a decent burial for the hound! Ifyour Warrior already hasa pet dog,rollonthistable again. Ifnot,nameit and noteit down on your Warrior's Adventure RecordSheet.

event.name: Lepers
event.rules: Lepers begging for food accost your Warrior. The lepers will approach your Warrior each day until your Warrior gives them food,untilyour Warrior departs for the nextAdventure,or until your Warrior contracts their horrid affliction. Each day the lepers approachtobegforfood,your Warrior mustroll2D6.Ifboth dice come up the same number your Warrior has contracted their foul disease and they suffer-1 Movement from nowuntiltheendof thenextAdventure.

event.name: Madman
event.rules: A deranged lunatic approaches your Warrior and lunges forward with a knife! Your Warrior deals him a fatal blow in a clear act of self-defense. Rummaging through the deceased man's belongings your Warrior realizes that the man's necklace bears the crest of Duke Hilderwhelm.Monthsago,the Duke's nephew wasrumouredtobemissingandthismustbehim!Roll1D6to see the result of your Warrior's actions: 1 The authorities are furious and accuse your Warrior of murder!Theyarethrownintojailandscheduledtobe executedin10days.Attheendofeachdaythat passes,including this one,roll 1D6.If the result is a 6,it means that a witness to the incident has come forward to exonerate your Warrior.YourWarrioristhen immediatelyescortedoutsidetheSettlementgates.If no witnesscomesforwardby theendof thetenthday, your Warrior is beheaded in the center of the Settlementbeforealarge,jeeringcrowd. 2-3 In order to avoid any public embarrassment abouthis deceased nephew's stateofmind,DukeHilderwhelm has your Warrior arrested on trumped up charges.He will only drop the charges if your Warrior agrees to pay afine and keep quiet about the entireincident.These negotiationstake1D6daysandcostyourWarrior 1D6x50gold.IfyourWarriordoesnothavetheamount (after selling any Equipment or Treasure to cover the cost)thentheDuketakeswhatevergoldtheyhaveand drops the charges. 4-5 The influential Duke Hil der w helm has your Warrior arrested claiming that your Warrior killed his nephew during aduel.Your Warrior spends 3 days in jail while authoritiesdetermineiftheduelwasproper. 6 DukeHilderwhelm,wellawareofhisnephew's deterioratedmentalstate,paysyourWarrior1D6x100 goldtokeepsilentabouttheentireincident. SE

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Mysterious Priest
event.rules: frail looking horseis offering comfort and solace to some of the more wretched inhabitants - or so it seems. As the wagon approaches your Warrior,roll1D6onthetablebelow: 1 Uponcom mandfrom the priest a nightmarish creature bursts from the wagon and assaults your Warrior who manages to dispatchit,butonly after it claws them repeatedly.Thepriestvanishesduringthestruggleand your Warrior im m ediatelybeginstofeelill,thevictim of a strange disease that the creature carried.They m ust spendthenext1D3daysinbeddoingnothingand must deduct1D6 Wounds from their Starting Wounds score permanently. The priest convinces your Warrior to trade one of their 2-3 weapons for one the priest claims is a holy artefact that will protect them from harm.Ifyour Warrior is the WarriorPriest,theBretonnian Knight,ortheSisterof Sigmar,theysenseeviland corruption and decline the offersteadfastly.AnyotherWarriormustroll1D6and addtheir Willpower.Ifthetotalis 8or more they refuse. If the total is less than 8theyreadily agree to the trade and must discard a weapon of theirchoice.The weapon traded for is the same type as the one given but it carriesa terrible curse. Inthe nextAdventure,eachtime anatural1isrolled in the Power Phase,yourWarrior mustdraw and use the cursed weapon.The weapononlyhitsonanatural6 anddoesDamageasnormal.However,ifhisToHitisa natural1,your Warrior uncontrollably lashes outand attacks the nearest Warrior instead. AtthenextSettlement,your Warrior may rid himself of the weapon and the curse by visiting a Temple and paying 1D6x100 gold. 4 The priest turns out to be a simple clergyman on his wayto anearbymonastery. 5 The priest is carrying food for the less fortunate and upon seeing the sorry state ofyour Warrior's tattered gear and items he offers to trade food in exchange for anyitem your Warrioriscarrying.Foreachitem of Equipment or Treasure that they give up,your Warrior is given 1D6 Provisions. 6 The priest tells your Warrior that he has seen them in a vision and that they willbecomea'starof theheavens'. Heplaces something in your Warrior's hand and then leads his horse and wagon away into the darkness. Theitem placedin his hand is a polished stone with a strangerune.OnceperAdventureyourWarriormay utilise the power within thestone which allows them to addanextra1D6to theirStrengthforoneCombat.

event.name: Healer
event.rules: A healeris passing through the makeshift camps outside the Settlements offering to cure any maladies at no charge. Roll 1D6 and if the score is a 5 or6,thehealerreallydoes have the power to heal and restores 1 point to one Characteristic (your choice)thathasbeen previouslyreducedforanyreason(such as Poison, etc.). On any other score,the healer blesses your Warrior by licking his thum b and then rubbing it across your Warrior's forehead before moving on.

event.name: Heavy Rains
event.rules: A heavy rainfall bombards the Settlement. With no placeto the constant downpour, your Warrior seek refuge from develops a terrible, hacking cough.During the nextDungeon, your Warrior suffers-1 To Hit until they drink a Healing Potion.

event.name: Young Whelp
event.rules: Your Warrior catches ayoung urchin trying to steal their purse. The boy claims that he's an orphan supporting himself and his sisterandbegsformercy.IfyourWarriorfreeshim,roll1D6: 1-2 ThecontemptuousyoungladpromisestoNEVER,EVER steal again and darts away.YourWarrior'sfearsare confirmed when they reach into their pack and discoversthatoneitemismissing(determined randomly). 3-6 Theboyfallstohiskneesinoverwhelmingthanks. Before departing he gives your Warriora piece of parchment that he lifted offofa merchant in the Settlement,explainingthathecan'tread anyway.Onit is the location of a secret cache of gold worth 1D6x100 gold.Ifthe Warriors defeat all of the Monsters in the Objective Room duringthenextAdventure,your Warrior may claim this treasure. Ifyour Warrior delivers him in to the Watchroll1D6: 1-3 The poor lad cannot withstand the severe beating receives from the authoritiesanddies,leavinghissister to careforherself.Accordingtolocallawsand customs your Warrior is now responsible for the girl's welfare. From now on,each time your Warrior reaches a Settlement,they mustsend1D6x50goldforherliving expenses. 4-6 The impudent child has been a huge menace and the Watch has been trying to apprehend the young whelp forthepastmonth.YourWarriorisgiven 1D6x25gold for catching the troublesome urchin.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Admittance
event.rules: In order to address the grumbling of the lower classes, the mayor of the Settlement has declared that a small handful of peasants willbe allowed to enter.Ifyour Warrior was restricted from entering the Settlement for any reason,they are one of the lucky few selected. They may re-enter the Settlement immediately if they so choose, paying Living Expenses and 7

event.name: Thrown Out
event.rules: Your Warrior's wild behaviour attracts the attention of the Settlement authorities and there are thrown outoftown.They must wait outside the Settlement gates for the other Warriors. In addition,roll1D6.On a score of1 your Warrior is fleeced by the gate guards asthey are thrown outand have alltheir gold stolen!

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Hunting
event.rules: Your Warrior is invited by a group of locals togo on a nocturnal hunt. Meeting them just before sunset,they are informed that tonight's prey is the great Qu arg a beast of fearsome temper andfouldisposition.Your Warrior's task in the group is to take the hunting tools- a net,a sm all pole with a bellon it and a bag full of garlic and wait in the middle of the woods while the rest of the hunters spread out into the forest and drive theQuarg towardsthem.Asthesunrisesthenextmorning,and theowls return to their nests, there is stillno sign of the Quarg,your Warrior begins to wonder if someone is being madeafoolof...

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Witchcraft
event.rules: Your Warrior is accused of witchcraft,andis chased through the streets by an angry mob. Roll1D6 on the following table (the Witch Hunter may add +1tohisscore): 1-3 Your Warrior escapes by jumping over the Settlement wall and into the filthy water of the moat.Pelted with eggsandrottenfruit,theystaggeroutonto thefar rules (page 3). 4-5 Your Warrior ducks down aside alley andescapes,but from now on must wear a disguise when out in public! 6 Your Warrior turns angrily on the mob,demandingan explanation.Shoutingdowntheleaderof the m ob,they establish theirauthorityoverthecrowd,who sheepishly returnto theirhovels,pausingonlytogive your Warrior 1o0goldas way of compensation.

event.name: Nightmare
event.rules: As your Warrior retires for the evening they fall into a deep slumber.Theybeginto dream that they are fighting a terrifying gorgon;herhaircomposedofvicioussnakesand agaze which can turn a person to stone. with a horrendous scream the gorgon attacks! Roll 1D6 to see how the dream ends: 1 No matter how many times or how hardyour Warrior strikes the creature with their weapon,the beast seemsinvulnerable.Withaterrifying scream the gorgon shootsan arrow directly into your Warrior's heart! Whenyour Warriorwakes,theyfindthat theyhave permanently lost 1 Wound!Ifyour Warrior's Starting Woundsiseverreducedtozerobecauseofthisdream, theyaredead. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must roll on this table again.MakeanoteonyourAdventure Record Sheet to remind you. 2-5 The battle is brutal and neither your Warrior nor the creatureseemstogettheupperhand.YourWarrior wakesin acold sweat. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must roll on this table again!MakeanoteonyourAdventure Record Sheet to remind you. Your Warrior courageously stands their ground and 6 strikes the wretchedbeastdown!A sense of relief fills your Warrior and they sleep better than they have in a long time. Whenyour Warrior awakens they discover that they are +1to his Starting Wounds!The nightmare ends and theydonothavetorollonthistableagain(unless theyencounterthisSettlementEventagain)! If your Warrior leaves the Settlement before the dream concludes, the nightmare temporarily ends. At the next Settlement, however,it returns to haunt the Warrior again. MakeanoteonyourAdventureRecordSheet.

event.name: Well
event.rules: Strolling alongside a dilapidated oldhouse your Warrior spies an old,stone well.Your Warrior casually tosses in 1 gold coin.Roll 1D6 on the table below: 1 Acreepy,gutturalmoanechoesupwardinanunknown tongue.A terrible sense of dread overwhelms your Warriorand theysuffer-1LuckforthenextAdventure. 2-5 Nothing happens and your Warrior is now carrying one less gold piece. 6 A high pitched squeal and soft giggling can be heard at the bottom of the well.Your Warrior gains +1Luck for next Adventure!

event.name: Festival
event.rules: Asyour Warriors trolls through the village centre it is abuzz with thepreparationsfortomorrow'sfestival.Roll1D6tosee what festival is being celebrated and another 1D6tosee what effect ithason ALL ofyour Warriors. 1D6 Festival A Beer festival 1 2 A Food festival A festival for the Deceased 3 4 A Religious festival A Music festival 5 6 A Cultural festival 1D6 Effect 1 In recognition of thefestival,alllocations willbe closed forthenext1D6 days.Warriorswhoaretrainingwill find their instruction interrupted during the period and they mustadd on those extra days to their training time. 2 In recognition of the festival, all traders will close up shopforthenext1D3days. 3 The population of the Village will double as it welcomes visitors from thesurroundingregion.Forthenext1D3 days all Warriors must roll on the Town Events Table instead of the Village Events Table.In addition, Warriors may make all stock rolls as if in a Town (2D6). 4 Vendors,merchantsandtraders will make their wayto the Village tomorrow and any Warrior may visitthe Peddler, who is treated exactly asthe Peddler from the Role play Book (page 15)orthenew,expanded Travelling Hazards by Little monk (page 8).Visitingthe Peddler takes an entire day during which a Warrior 5 In celebration (but mostly to lure in new customers)all locations will offer one item in stock at5o%.Thissale is offered tomorrow only. 6 In celebration (but m ostly tolurein customers)all locations willoffer1o% of fall items in stock.Thissale is offered tomorrow only! If any Warrior rolls this Settlement Event again during this stay,re-rollthis result.

event.name: Family Heirloom
event.rules: An item of Treasure that your Warrior is carrying (determined randomly)isidentifiedbya wealthy,influentialmemberof the local citizenry ashisfamilyheirloom,stolenlastyearby wanton burglars.The local Watch confiscates theitem and you must discarditimmediately.If your Warrior has no Treasure items, the wealthy local accuses your Warrior of hiding it and demands 1D6x50 goldin restitutioninstead.Ifthey cannotpay,then your

event.name: Runaway
event.rules: While walking along the main street, your Warrior hears a disturbance just around the corner.A moment later a huge runaway bull charges down thestreet, wrecking market stalls andcausingpanic.YourWarriorcanletitpassbyhidingdowna side alley, or try to stop the enraged animal.If your Warrior hides down the alley and lets the bullpass, roll 1D6 on the following table: 1-3 YourWarriorskulksin theshadows,waitingforthe actiontodiedown.Astheypeep roundthecorner,a mugger's metal bar hits them overthe head.Whenthey regain consciousness,your Warrior's purse is 1oo gold coins lighter. 4-6 After waiting nervously in the dark forafew minutes, your Warrior sees the bull roar past and career off downasidestreet.Thedangerispassedandyour Warrior may carry on about their business. Ifyour Warrior tries to stopthe bull,roll1D6 on the following table: 1-2 Your Warrior waves the passers by aside and leaps out infrontof thebull,sword drawn.The bull hesitates for halfa secondorso,and thentramplesthem into the dirt before carrying on its way.The crowd then stomps all over your Warrior in the rush to follow the bull, leaving them to nurse their injuries alonein an empty street. 3-4 A single sword thrust stops the bull dead in its tracks literally.Asitscarcasstwitchesspasmodically,the crowd roars in approval and showers your Warrior with 100 gold.Thenthebull'senragedownerarrives,and relieves them of150gold-it washisprizestud,and he wanteditstopped,notslaughtered! 5-6 Your Warrior gives the bulla hard stare and its kids to a halt,snortingandpawingtheground.Puttingarope arounditsneck,theyleaditbacktoitsstall.Thecrowd cheers and showers them with150gold.

event.name: Illness
event.rules: A terrible illness strikes the Settlem ent, and your W arrior falls preytoit.Theymustspend thenexttwodaysinbed,paying10 gold per day for medication as well as the normal Living Expenses.Whileyour Warrior is in bed,youdonothavetoroll

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Temple Donation
event.rules: Much taken with the teachings of thelocalpriest,your Warrior pledges 1D6x50 gold to his cause, selling equipment if necessary to meetthecostof thedonation.Thenexttime your Warrior is in a fight and fails to hit their opponent,the sect's only do this once foreach donation they make (i.e.,each time they roll this result on this table).

event.name: River Monster
event.rules: Your Warrior overhears that the local fisherman are com plaining ofa giant aquatic monster that has been terror ising them down by the river. Upon investigation your Warrior discovers a massive creature thathas somehow managed to findits way inland.Aftera few patient hours your Warrior spots the beast anda smallbattleforwaterrightsensues.Roll1D6onthetable below: 1 ThestrangecreatureistoomuchforyourWarrior. Razor sharp teeth and a piercing snout tear at your Warrior and soonthey are running from the river at top speed,muchto thechagrin of thelocals.Yourbested Warriorsuffers-1ToughnessforthenextAdventure. 2-3 The mighty fish tosses your Warrior about and leaves them on the bank in a heap.Theirhandis badly injured in the struggle and they suffer-1 Weapon Skill for the next Adventure. 4-5 Your Warrior thrashes about in the water as the creature snaps and stabs at them with its long duck likebill.Eventually your Warrior drives the creature away and returns to the applauding fishermen who thank them with1D6x25 gold. 6 As the giant fish leaps outof the water,yourWarrior fellsit withone swift and accurate blow.The fishermen are ecstatic as they haul the beast out of the water. They regard your Warrior as a hero and givethem 1D6x50 gold.Inaddition,theyusethecreatureto prepare1D6 ProvisionsforyourWarrior.Treatthem like normal Provisions from the General Store.

event.name: Snake
event.rules: As your Warrior passes bya potter'sstore,a woman comes running out hollering about a poisonous snake that has slipped in. She immediately hires your Warrior to rid hershop of the snake,butshe warns them notto break any of her pottery which is on display throughout the shop.Roll1D6below: 1 The snake is much harder to catch or kill than anticipated and your Warrior practically tears the shop apart before finally corralling thereptile.Angrily,the shopownerdemands1D6x50goldforherloses. 2-3 Justas your Warriors lips the snag into a burlap sack, they stumble backward into a rare and expensive pot which falls to the ground withaloudcrash.After deducting their pay for catching the snake,your Warrior owesthepotter1D6x10goldforthedamage. 4-5 Stepping into the potter's shop your Warrior spies the cold blooded creature coiled upin therafters.They manageto bring it down along with afew cups hanging onthe wall.Thepotteris elated and,afterdeductingfor damage,paysyour Warrior1D6x10gold. 6 No sooner than your Warrior enters the shop do they pounce upon the terrifying reptile,snatchingit with their bare hands.Whenthey bringthe beastie back out, the potter is elated that nothing wasdamaged.She paysyour Warrior1D6x25goldfor theirservices. 10

event.name: Accident
event.rules: As your Warrior helps an old crone to cross the busy main street,a beer wagon unexpectedly crashes into them. She carefully peels them offthe heavy,iron-shed wheel,andtakes them to the infirmary to be patched up. Your Warrior cannot buy anything or visit any Special Locations for 1D6 days while they recover. While they are recovering you do not have to roll

event.name: Pacifist
event.rules: Seeing them armed andbattle-hardened,apacifistapproaches your Warrior,attemptingtoconvertthem to the ways ofnon- violence.Theyounglady is passionate and convincing and you mustroll1D6 and add your Warrior's Willpower.Look upthe result on the table below to see what effect her words have on your W arrior. 2-4 Your Warrior takes the words of the woman to heart and they vow to relinquish one of their weapons(your choice).Discarditim mediately. 5-6 Your Warrior is thoroughly convinced by the wordsof theyoung woman and in the next Adventure they promisethat,asidefrom theirregularAttacks,they will not use any special skills or abilities that cause Damage. 7-8 Your Warrior deeply considers the words of the young woman and in the next Dungeon they refuse to make an Attack in the first Warriors ' Phase ofeach combat. Your Warrior is unaffected by the words of the young 9or lady. more

event.name: Pickpocket
event.rules: As your Warrior rushes through the busy streets,theirmoney pouch is stolen. YourWarriorloses1D6x20 gold.

event.name: Betrothed
event.rules: Due to a mistake in identity, your Warrior finds himself are betrothed to marrytheirsister.Your Warrior agrees,albeit at knifepoint,andpledgestheirtroth to a farmer's daughter of a m ple charm s. The marriage is arranged fortomorrow,so your Warrior must either leave the Settlement im mediately,orget married...

event.name: Friendly Villager
event.rules: As your Warrior heads back to the inn for a bit of relaxation, they encounter a villager who welcomes them to the Settlement heartily and insists on taking them to the nearest tavern for a drink.Roll1D6 on the following tables: 1D6 ThepersonistheVillage... 1 Idiot Drunk 2 3 Loudmouth 4 Bard Gate Guard 5 Elder 6 Nowrollanother1D6andaddthenumberrolledabove.Look uptheresultonthetablebelow: Result Total 2-3 Upon entering thetavern,yourWarrioris im mediate ly surrounded byruffians.Theyfind out that the villager owes a huge debt to the owner of the establishment and he was told not to return until hisdebt waspaidinfull.Beforebeingthrown out with theirnew friend,your Warrior is relieved of 1D6x50gold.Iftheydonothavethatamount,the owner takes one item ofTreasure(determinedat random)ascompensation. 4-5 As your Warrior struts into the tavern with the villageritisapparentthat theyarenotwelcomehere. Afterbeingroughedupandthrownout,yourWarrior finds outthat one of the enforcers made off with one item of Equipment(determined atrandom).Discard itim mediately. 6-8 Your Warrior spends the next few hours politely listening to the boring history of the settlement after whichthe villager leaves them to foot the bill, which amountsto1D6x10gold. As your Warrior and the villager walk through the 9-10 door,theotherpatronscheerfully greet them (the villager is apparently a bit of a celebrity).Your Warriorand thevillageraregivenfreefoodanddrink. Your Warrior does not need to pay for living expenses today. 11-12 The villager is well known and wellrespected.Seeing your Warrior with him,thevillagersin thetavernpay allof the Living Expenses for the rest of their stay in theSettlementanddonate1D6x25goldtowardtheir next Adventure.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Circus
event.rules: Thereis a travelling circusintown,and your Warrior spends the rest of their day wanderingfromsideshowtosideshow.Having metthebeardedwomanand thetwo-headedgoat,theydecide tohavetheirfortuneread.Perhapsthe sign above the door to that particular wagon should say Have your fortune stolen',as for a cost of 1D6x10 gold,your Warrior is told nothing more than that their destiny lies with a tall, dark stranger from Erengrad!

event.name: Gambling
event.rules: Your Warrior visits a localinn and gets involved in a dubious gameofdice.Roll1D6onthefollowingtable: Your Warriorlosesbadly,and must forfeit one item of 1 Treasure of your choice. 2 YourWarriorloses1D6x20gold. 3-4 YourWarriorcomesoutof thegameeven. Your Warriorwins1D6x10gold. 5 Your Warrior wins3D6x10gold. 6

event.name: Beggars
event.rules: Your Warrior is accosted by beggars and,overwhelmed by the sadness of theirplight,gives each of the1D6 pitiful wretches5 gold.Ifyour Warriorcannotpaytheyareabeggarthemselves, and you m ustroll1D6 on the following table: 1 Your Warrior is thrown out of the Settlement see VillageEvent11. Your Warriorgains1D6x5goldbybegging. 2-6

event.name: Pugilist
event.rules: As you wander through the Village your Warrior comes upon some bare knuckle pugilists competing with one another. Catching sight of your Warrior's garb and equipment they im mediate ly hailyou tojoin them in a friendly bout,albeit with much public jeering and taunting.Your Warrior has no choice buttoupholdtheirreputationbyacceptingthelocals'challenge. Stepping up to meet your Warrior is Bam wig,localchampion and prize fighter. The protocols for such a bout here are unfamiliar to your Warrior so they are given alist of rules and regulations that they must adhere to. Add your Warrior's Strength and Toughness together and then factor in their Alehouse modifier (see your Warrior's Rulebook). This is your Warrior's Fight Total. The local champion's Fight Tota/is8. Eachroundyoumustrolla1D6foryour Warrior and another 1D6forBamwig.Deduct your Warrior's score from their Fight Totaland then deduct Bam wig's roll from his. Keep a running total on a piece of paperand the first contender whose Fight Total reaches zero (orbelow)is theloser.Ifboth contestants ' score reach zero or below atthe same time the higher score prevails.In case ofa tie, continue another round until one of them is the winner. If your Warrior wins the match they receive one item of Dungeon Room Treasure. If they are defeated, the local champion and his cohorts relieve them of half their gold!

event.name: Inheritance
event.rules: A head villager approaches your Warrior and in forms them that a distant relative hasjust passed away and has left them an inheritance.Roll1D6 tosee what the inheritance is: 1 The inheritance is a debt and the head villager has come tocollect!Your Warrior mustforfeit1D6x25 gold selling Equipment and Treasure if necessary! 2-3 The inheritance is actually apet dog(seeEvent63).If your W arrior already has a pet dog,treat this as a roll of 1 above. 4 The inheritance is a small sum of money to the amount of 1D6x25 gold! The inheritance is a small sum of money to the amount 5 of1D6x50 gold! 6 The inheritance is anitem of Dungeon Room Treasure!

event.name: Fooled
event.rules: Your Warrior must discard anyone purchase they have madein this Settlement,as it was a fake and is worth nothing!Ifyour Warrior has not yet made any purchases in this Settlement, treat this as an Uneventful Day.

event.name: Riotous Living
event.rules: Taken with the joys of hot food and a comfortable bed afterso long outin the wild,your Warrior overspends on such luxuries by30 gold.

event.name: An Honest Day's Work
event.rules: Your Warrior is employed by a local farmer to carry bales of cotton to the riverside area forthe day.This task earns them 20 gold.

event.name: Fire
event.rules: As your Warrior passes by a small house they see smoke seeping out from underthefrontdoor.Rushinginsidetheydrag outthe unconsciousinhabitants.Almostthe entire village turns outto fight the blaze and the house is saved with no casualties. Your Warrior is being congratulated as a hero,especially from the surviving family who makea giftof 1D6x20 gold in thanks.

event.name: Troubadour
event.rules: A colour fully dressed minstrel notices your Warrior as they pass by. With an exasperated laugh he follows your Warrior,loudly strum ming hislute and singing aballad allabout your Warrior's exploits.Roll1D6: 1-2 The raucous tune is an insulting account of all of your Warrior'sfollies;from their missteps in combatto spilling their drink atthetavern.Thebard also singsof the unfortunate encounter with the miller's daughter down by the river which angers some of her close male relatives.They waitfor your Warrior around acorner, throw a sack over their head and beat them severely. Your Warriorsuffers-1 Strength forthe next Adventure. 3-4 The ballad is all about your Warrior's exploits but no one seemsto even notice orcare. 5-6 The bard embellishes all of your Warrior's good deeds and heroics to such degree that even your Warrior is impressed with himself.Alargegroup quickly gathers and givesthreecheers!Your Warrior's inner spirit is bolsteredand theyare+1Strengthforthenext Adventure.

event.name: Pet Dog
event.rules: YourWarriorisadoptedbyasmalldog.Itfollowsthem around everywhere,skulkingin theshadows while the fighting goes on and then emerging after the adventure is completed to shower its new master withadoration.RollonthePetDog Event Table (page 24) to see exactly which type of canine has attached itselftoyou. Your Warrior can killthe dogif you want,butit wouldbea cruel and heartless thingto doandit will cost them 1,ooo gold to pay for a decent burial for the hound! Ifyour Warrior already has a pet dog,ignorethis result and roll again on the Village Events table.Ifnot,nameitand noteit downonyourWarrior'sAdventureRecordSheet.

event.name: Crime
event.rules: Your Warrior is accused of murder and thrown in jail. The matter is sorted out eventually, but only after your Warrior's companions have bailed them out.Each Warrior in the party except the accused mustpay 1D6x5 gold to the authorities. Iftheir companions do not have the money,your Warrior mustspend1D6 daysinjail.Whileinjail they don't have to pay

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Fearful Villagers
event.rules: Your Warrior is carrying an item of magical Treasure (determined randomly) that the villagers perceive as unlucky, unholy ortaboo.ThelocalWatchisim mediate ly summoned and your Warrior must either leave the Settlement im mediate ly or surrender the item to the authorities (after which it is properly destroyed in a cerem ony involving a bonfire,a catapult,a ten poundhammerandalargesquash).

event.name: Thrown Out
event.rules: Your Warrior's wild behaviour attracts the attention of the Settlement authorities and there are thrown out oftown.They must wait outside the Settlement gates for the other Warriors. In addition,roll1D6.On ascore of 1 your Warrior is fleeced goldstolen!

event.name: Well
event.rules: Strolling alongside a dilapidated old house your Warrior spies an old,stone well.Your Warrior casually tosses in 1 gold coin. Roll 1D6onthetablebelow: 1 Acreepy,guttural moan echoes upward in an unknown tongue.Aterriblesenseofdreadoverwhelmsyour Warrior and they suffer-1 Luck for the next Adventure. 2-5 Nothing happens and your Warrior is now carrying one less gold piece. 6 A high pitched squeal and soft giggling can be heard at the bottom of the well.Your Warrior is +1Luckforthe next Adventure!

event.name: Debt
event.rules: A debt collector catches up with your Warrior, demanding payment for money borrowed yearsago.Thesum your Warrior borrowed was not substantial, but the interest rates are astronomical. The outstanding balance is 1D6x20 gold,andif your Warrior cannot pay in fullim mediate ly the debt collector takes alltheir gold plus one piece of Equipment or Treasure (determinedrandomly).

event.name: Investment
event.rules: In one of the wharf side bars,your Warrior meets a young to cover the known world.Hetalksyour Warrior into investing 1D6x50 gold, giving them a certificate of partnership. From now on,at the end of each Dungeon, whenever your Warrior reaches civilisation,they may visitthe local Merchants ' Guild and show them their deed of partnership. Consulting their records,they can tell your Warrior how the company is doing. Roll1D6 on the following table: 1 Thecompanyhasgonebust,andyourWarrior's investment is worthnothing.Inaddition,they mustpay themerchants1D6x50 goldin outstanding debts. 2-5 Your Warrior's investment is performing fairly well,and the merchant forwards them 1D6x10 gold as their share of the profits. 6 Businessisbooming!Afterconsultingtheledger,the merchant confirms that he can forward your Warrior 1D6x50gold. artisans,traders and such.Unfortunately,that also means and more spread out than villages, many more things can ead ofevents and encounters. However,townsfolk can still heir step and not upsetthelocals...

event.name: Fight

FIGHT Your W arrior argues with a burly street trader who has tried to charge them fartoo muchforsomecheap,shoddyitems.The argument escalates to a brawl very quickly as the trader calls in hisguards.Roll1D6 on the following table: Your Warriorissoundlythrashedandloses2oogold. 1 2 Your Warrior is bested after a hard struggle against the traderandhiscroniesandloses1oogold. Your Warrior walks away withabloodynoseand50 3 gold worse off,butleavesthetraderbadlybruised. 4 Afterafiercestruggle,your Warrior knocks the impudent trader to theground,wherehestays to collecthisteeth.Whileheisbusy,yourWarriortakes 100 goldfrom his stall as compensation. YourWarriorquicklydisarmsthetrader.Withasword 5 athisthroat,thelouthasno choice but to apologise and offer your Warrior2oo gold as compensation. 6 A dozen evil looking thugs the trader's bodyguards leapoutfrom theshadows,attackingyour Warrior with knives,coshes and cudgels.Unperturbed,they coolly dispatch them with afew well placed sword strokes, emptiestheirpurses,andendsup3o0 gold better off.

event.name: Nightmare
event.rules: As your Warrior retires for the evening they fallinto a deep slumber.They beginto dream that they are fighting a terrifying gorgon;herhaircomposedofvicioussnakesand agaze which can turn a person to stone. with a horrendous scream the gorgon attacks! Roll 1D6 to see how the dream ends: 1 No matter how many times or how hard your Warrior strikes the creature with their weapon,the beast seems invulnerable. With a terrifying scream the gorgonshootsanarrowdirectlyinto theirheart! WhenyourWarriorwakes,theyfindthat theyhave permanentlylost1 Wound!Ifyour Warrior's Starting Woundsiseverreducedtozerobecauseofthisdream, theyare dead. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must roll on this table again.Makeanote on your Adventure Record Sheet to remind you. 2-5 The battle is brutal and neither your Warrior nor the creature seems to gettheupperhand.Your Warrior wakesina coldsweat. Tomorrow night the nightmare returns and they must rollonthistableagain!MakeanoteonyourAdventure Record Sheet to remind you. 6 Your Warrior courageously stands their ground and strikes the wretchedbeastdown!A sense of relief fills your Warrior and they sleep better than they have in a long time. When your Warrior awakens they discover that they are +1tohisStarting Wounds!Thenightmareends and they do not have to roll on this table again (unless theyencounterthisSettlementEventagain)! If your Warrior leaves the Settlement before the dream concludes, the nightmare temporarily ends. At the next Settlement,however,it returns to haunt the Warrior again. MakeanoteonyourAdventureRecordSheet.

event.name: Drugged
event.rules: Visiting a local hostelry in a rather seedy area, your W arrior partakes of a curious drink offered them by the bartender. Afterone swig,theyfeelthe effects of the liquid take hold- they have been drugged! Roll1D6 on the following table: Your Warriorhasbeenpoisonedandsuffers-1 1-3 Toughness for the duration of the next Adventure. 4-6 The liquid in question is simply very strong ale,and your Warrior quickly getsatasteforit. The only ill effect is a pounding head next morning.

event.rules: Your Warrior spends the rest of their day in the heady atmosphere of a steam bath,sweating offthe aftereffects of last night's sojourn at the tavern. They emerge feeling far healthierthantheyhaveforagoodwhile,and theymayadd+1 Wound to their Starting Wounds score permanently.

event.name: Overturned Cart
event.rules: Your Warrior hears a loud cry from for help from around the cart has turned over andis crushingalocalboy.Your Warrior runs overto try and push the cart off the young lad.Roll1D6 and add your Warrior's Strength then look up the result below: 6 or Asyour Warriorliftsthecartuptheylosetheirgrip! less Thecartfallsbackonto theboy,maiminghim!The boy's family demands restitution for the accident and your Warrior m ust give up half their gold or besentto prisonfor1D6 weeks!IfyourWarriorhasnogold,they must give the family 1D3 TreasureItems(yourchoice) instead. 7 Your Warrior manages to movethecart,buttheboyis injuredin theprocess.YourWarriorfeelssoterrible aboutthe situation that theygive1D6x50 goldto cover thecost of medical treatment. 8-9 Your Warrior shifts the heavy cart enough for someone to pull the boy out,thussaving hislife.Hisfather,a localmerchant,isso grateful that he gives your Warrior 1D6x50gold. 10+ In aheroicfeat,your Warrior hauls the massive cartoff theboyandheescapesunscathed.Theonlookers cheer buoyantly and theboy'sfamily,who arerich nobles,givesyourWarrior1D6x100goldandanItem of Dungeon Room Treasure ingratitude!

The price the woman is asking is 1D6x1o0 gold and your Warrior may buythe weapon if they wish bypaying the gold and then rolling on the table below: 1 The sword functions as a regular sword from the Weaponsmith,butitissopoorlyconstructedthat the first time the Warriorrollsa1 To Hitin combat,the blade shatters and must be discarded. 2 The sword functions as a regular sword from the Weaponsmith,butisabitheavierandnotas well balanced.Thus,the wieldersuffers-1Initiative when wielding this sword in combat. 3 The sword is actually the Bronze Sigil Sword and gives the wielder +2Initiative. 4 The sword is actually the Sword of Might and givesthe wielder+1Strength. 5 The sword is actually the Blade of Leaping Copper and givesthe wielder+1Attacks. 6 The sword is actually the Heart seekerS word and once per Turn your Warrior may re roll anyone of their Attacks that misses.

event.rules: Your Warrior is employed by a local apothecary to unload suppliesathisshopfortheday.Thistask earns them 35 gold.

event.name: Robbery
event.rules: Your Warrior bumps into a thief who hasjust robbed the jeweller'sstore.Roll1D6 on the table below: 1 The culprit escapes as the shop owner emerges and accuses your Warrior of thetheft!Heim mediate ly summons the Watch who throw your Warrior in jail for 1D3daysandfinethem 1D6x100gold! 2-3 TherobberthrowssomejewelsatyourWarrior,yelling Townsfolk come running out of the surroundings buildings and surround yourWarrior,screamingforthe Watch.Afterrepeatedpleasofinnocence,yourWarrior isfined 1D6x50 goldforfailingtostopthecrime. 4-5 Your Warrior instinctively knocks the culprit down, forcing him to drop allofhisloot.The nimble thief manages to regain his feet and climb over a wall and outofsight.Alocalwhowitnessedeverything corroborates your story to the shopkeeper and the Watch whentheyarrive.Theownerrewardsyour Warrior with 1D6x25 gold for saving his merchandise. 6 Your Warriorgrabsthethief,slamshim to the floor and sitson him untilthe Watch arrive.Theshopkeeperlauds your Warrior as a hero and rewards them with1D6x50 gold!Inaddition,theyare given a bronze pendant with an ancient rune offortuneinscribeduponit.Eachtime your Warrior visitsa Settlement the pendant allows you tore roll one SettlementEvent.You must accept the result of the secondroll.

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Hunting
event.rules: Your Warrior is invited by a group of locals togo on a nocturnal hunt. Meeting them just before sunset,they are informed that tonight's prey is the great Qu arg a beast of fearsome temper andfouldisposition.Your Warrior's task in the group is to take thehuntingtools-anet,asmallpolewithabellonitandabag fullof garlic and waitin the middle of the woods while the rest of the hunters spread out into the forest and drive the Quarg towardsthem.Asthesunrisesthenextmorning,and theowls return to their nests, there is still no sign of the Quarg,your Warrior begins to wonder if someone is being made afoolof...
