event.rules: As your Warrior rushes through the busystreets,theirmoney pouch is stolen. YourWarriorloses1D6x30gold.

event.name: Runaway
event.rules: While walking along the main street, your Warrior hears a disturbance just around the corner.A moment later a huge runaway bull charges down thestreet,wrecking market stalls andcausingpanic.Your Warrior can let it pass by hiding down a side alley, or try to stop the enraged animal.If your Warrior hides down the alley and lets the bullpass,roll1D6 onthe following table: 1-3 Your Warriorskulksin theshadows,waitingforthe action todiedown.Astheypeep roundthecorner,a mugger's metal bar hits them overthe head. Whenthey regain consciousness,your Warrior's purse is 1oo gold coins lighter. After waiting nervously in the dark forafew minutes, 4-6 your Warrior sees the bull roar past and career off downasidestreet.Thedangerispassedandyour Warriormaycarryonabouttheirbusiness. Ifyour Warrior tries to stop the bull,roll1D6 on the following table: 1-2 Your Warrior waves the passers by aside and leaps out infrontof thebull,sword drawn.Thebullhesitatesfor half a second orso,and thentramplesthem into the dirtbeforecarryingonitsway.Thecrowdthenstomps all over your Warrior in the rush to follow thebull, leaving them to nurse their injuries alonein an empty street. 3-4 A single sword thrust stops the bull dead in its tracks literally.Asits carcasstwitchesspasmodically,the crowd roars in approval and showers your Warrior with 100gold.Thenthebull'senragedownerarrives,and relieves them of150 gold-itwashisprizestud,and he wanteditstopped,notslaughtered! 5-6 Your Warrior gives the bulla hard stare and its kids to a halt,snorting andpawingtheground.Putting arope arounditsneck,theyleaditback toitsstall.The crowd cheers and showers them with150gold.

event.name: Jealous Adventurer
event.rules: Unbeknownst to them, your Warrior is being watched and followed bya young man. The stranger is a failed adventurer and he longs for the fame and glory that your Warrior seems to haveobtained.Hejealously decides to sabotage your Warrior's achievements. In the next Dungeon it becomes clear that someone has alerted the denizens to the Warriors ' coming. Each time an Unexpected Event occurs that reveals Monsters,you mustroll 1D6.On a score of 1-3 you must roll twice on the Monster Table with the minimum amountof Monstersarriving.On ascoreof 4-6you rollonly once onthe Monster Table as normal,butthe maximum numberof Monsters arrive.

event.name: Join The Watch
event.rules: The Captain of the Watch marks your Warrior as a useful looking recruit and,ashe is short on manpower,press gangs them fora week.Your Warrior can either try to escape the Watch by making a donation to their funds of 2D6x10 gold,or join up. If your Warrior joins up they do not have to pay Living Expenses for the weekandearns2ogoldin wages.However, while in the Watch they have no time to visit any traders or Special Locations.

event.name: Salesman
event.rules: A salesman has set up shop just outside an inn selling Holy Water.He offers it to your Warrior claiming thatit will protect themfrom evilspiritsandcanevendestroytheundead. You m ay buy up to 1D6 Holy Water vials at the cost of 1D6x50 gold each. After purchasing the vials,the salesman takes downhis wares and disappears into theinn.Nowrollfor each holy watervialpurchased.Onascore of1 or2,the vialis filled with simple drinking water and is useless. On any other score,the vial actually does contain a magical holy water, purified and blessed by a priest.If drunk, the imbibe r is protected by a magical aura which lasts for one Turn and all attacks against the drinker made by Undead Monsters do half the normalDamage(rounded down).If thrown atan Undead Monster rollon your Ballistic Skill,each successful hit causing1D6 Wounds with no deductions for anything.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Thrown Out
event.rules: Your Warrior's wild behaviour attracts the attention of the Settlement authorities and there are thrown outoftown.They must wait outside the Settlement gates for the other Warriors. In addition,roll1D6.On a score of1 your Warrior is fleeced by the gate guards asthey are thrown outand have alltheir gold stolen!

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Investment
event.rules: In one of the wharf side bars, your Warrior meets a young entrepreneur with great ideas for establishing a trading empire tocover the Known World.Hetalksyour Warrior into investing 1D6x5o gold per Battle-level, giving them a certificate of purchase. From now on,atthe end of the Dungeon,wheneveryour Warrior reaches civilisation,they may visit the local Merchant's Guild andshow them theirdeedofpartnership(thistakesone day). Consulting their records,they can tellyour Warrior how the companyisdoing.Roll1D6 on the following table: 1 Thecompanyhasgonebust,andyourWarrior's investment is worthnothing.In addition,they mustpay the merchants1D6x50goldperBattle-levelin outstanding debts. 2-5 Your Warrior's investment is performing fairly well and the merchant forward them 1D6x10goldperBattle- level as their share of the profits. Businessisbooming!Afterconsultingtheledger,the 6 merchant confirms that he can forward your Warrior 1D6x50 goldperBattle-level.

event.name: Pet Dog
event.rules: Your Warriorisadoptedbyasmalldog.Itfollowsthem around everywhere,skulkingin theshadows while the fighting goes on and then emerging after the adventure is completed to shower its new master with adoration.Rollonthe Pet Dog Event Table (page 24) to see exactly which type of canine has attached itself to you. Your Warrior can killthe dogifyou want,butitwouldbe a cruel and heartless thing to doandit will cost them 1,ooo gold topayforadecentburialforthehound! Ifyour Warrior already hasapetdog,ignore this event and roll again on the City Events table.Ifnot, name it and noteit downon your Warrior's Adventure Record Sheet. 18 llfind a whole array ofthings to do! There willbe more more chances of something great happening butalso more a in ment and more people to meet.A crow dedcity willalso t m ore...

event.name: Cutpurse
event.rules: As your Warrior rushes through the busy streets, their money pouch is stolen. YourWarriorloses1D6x50gold.

event.name: Bathhouse
event.rules: Your Warrior spends the rest of their day in the heady atm o sphere of a steam bath,sweating off the aftereffects of last night's sojourn at the tavern. They emerge feeling far healthierthantheyhaveforagoodwhile,and theymayadd+1 Wound to their Starting Wounds scorepermanently.Inaddition, they are +1Initiativeforthe next Adventure.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: An Honest Day's Work?
event.rules: Your Warrior encounters a nervous looking merchant who is in a bitofa bind and asksforhelp.Someofhis workers have failed toshow up for work and he has a lot of crates that need to be transported to the warehouse district. Roll 1D6 on the table below: 1 The crates contain stolen goods and the Watch has had themerchantundersuspicionforsometime.Theentire group, including your Warrior, is arrested. Your Warrior mustspendthenext2 days in jail during which they are cleared of any wrong doing.Unfortunately,they fail to getpaid. 2-3 Your Warrior does not realise that the crates are filled with illegal substances and is surprised when the Watch arrives to arrest the merchant.Obviously,your Warrior receives no pay. 4-5 Your Warrior works diligently andis paid 50 goldfor a hard day's work. 6 Your Warrior's efforts are exemplary and the task earns them 1oo gold.

event.name: Riotous Living
event.rules: Taken with the joys of hot food and a comfortable bed after so long outin the wild,your Warrior overspends on such luxuries by 50 gold.

event.name: Hunting
event.rules: Your Warrior is invited by a group of locals to goon a nocturnal hunt. Meeting them just before sunset,they are informed that tonight's prey is the great Qu arg a beast of fearsome temper andfouldisposition.Your Warrior's task in the group is to take thehuntingtools-anet,asmallpolewithabellonitandabag full of garlic and wait in the middle of the woods while the rest of the hunters spread out into the forest and drivethe Quarg towardsthem.Asthesunrisesthenextmorning,and theowls return to their nests, there is stillno sign of the Quarg,your Warrior begins to wonder if someone is being made afoolof...

event.name: Street Artists
event.rules: Your Warrior is captivated by a particular brilliant team of street artists, who are performing their own rendition of a popular and contemporary play. The play your Warrior is watching iscalled (rollonce on each of thetablesbelow): 1D6 Result 1 Death of... The Lost Love of... 2 A Return To... 3 4 No Solace For... Cursed Be... 5 6 Drunken As... 1D6 Result 1 ...theEmperor. ...aLove Struck Wastrel! 2 3 ...Solitude. ...the Creeping Dead. 4 5 ...the Sylvan ian Counts. ...the Ever hungry Halfling! 6 Rollanother1D6toseehow good the actors were: 1 Appalling! Your Warrior has never seen such rubbish in their life and pelts the unfortunate actors with rotten vegetables! 2-5 Entertaining! Your Warrior gives the actors 10 gold x the total of the numbers rolled on the tables above. 6 Splendid! Your Warrior is captivated and showers the actors with well-earnedpraiseandalotofgoldtoo! Your Warrior gives the actors5o gold xthe numbers rolled on the tables above.

event.name: Counterfeit
event.rules: As your Warrior attempts to buy someequipment,the trader points out their gold is counterfeit and refuses to take it. Looking through their stash, your Warrior discovers that 1D6x10 of their gold is indeed worthless. Discard it and crossit off your Adventure Record Sheet. Ifyour Warrior does nothavethatmuchgoldleft,andhas already spent some in theSettlement,they must leave forthe nextDungeonimmediatelybeforeanyonecatcheson.

event.name: False Accusation
event.rules: Studying the weapons on display in the local smithy, your Warrior feels a steel gauntlet ed hand upon their shoulder.Itis the Marshal of the Watch and your Warrior is accused ofacrime they did not com mit! Your Warrior is arrested and thrown uponthe mercy of the courts. Your Warrior must roll once on each table below to see what crime they are accused ofand what their sentence is. 1D6 Crime 1 Trouble making 2 Robbery 3 Forgery 4 Arson Treachery 5 6 Murder Sentence 1D6 1 Innocent A stern warning! 2 3 1000 gold fine Lose a limb* 4 10 years in jail** 5 Deathby(rollontheExecution MethodTable) 6 1D6 Execution Method 1 Burning 2 Beheading 3 Impalement Drowning 4 5 Torture 6 Firing Squad *Lose a Limb The authorities decide to chop offone ofyour Warrior's limbs as punishment. You may choose which.If theylose aleg,your Warrior's Movement is halved and their Escape from Pinning roll is always a 6+.Ifthey everlose another leg they maynot move atall. If they lose a hand,thentheyare-2 onallTo Hit rolls and they may neveruse missile weaponsorcastspells.Iftheyever lose another hand they may not fight at all. **10YearsinJail This effectively means your Warrior is out of the game (as indeed does ' sentenced to death by...'.However,atthe start of every Adventure from now on,roll1D6.Onascoreof5or6 your Warrior escapes,together with all their gear, and may rejoin their fellow Warriors. Buying Your Freedom Ifthe sentence is a 4,5 or 6,the arrested Warrior(and other Warriors) can bribe the guards and the court officials in orderto avoidharm.Thecostofthis will be found by adding together all of the dice rolls from the Crime/Sentence/Execution Method tablesandmultiplyingtheresultby1oo.Thatishow muchgold it will cost for your Warrior to w alkfree,unharmed. 19

event.name: Wrestler
event.rules: "The Slammer" has come to town and he is taking on all challengers in a 'friendly' wrestling match! Boasting a perfect record,theNorseman wagers5oogoldpiecestoanyonein the crowd who defeats him in unarmed combat. At the urgingof the crowd your Warrior accepts the challenge,roll2D6 and apply their Alehouse modifier (see your Warrior's Rulebook). Look uptheresultonthetablebelow: 2-3 In the first few seconds,TheSlammerpicksyour Warrior up and promptly smashes them to the ground. The sickening thud ofyour Warrior hitting the ground sendsashudderthroughthecrowd.Momentslater your Warrioriscartedoffonawoodenplank.Notonly isyour Warrior5o0gold pieceslighter,butThe Slam mer helps himself to oneitem of Treasure (determined atrandom)your Warrior was carrying. 4-5 Aftera nifty maneuver,The Slam mergrabs your Warrior in a bear hug that feels like they're being squeezed by...well,abear. With eyes bulging your Warrior gives up just prior to slipping into unconsciousness. With terribly bruised ribs and an even more bruised ego yourWarriorforfeits5o0gold. 6-8 After a grueling 15 minutes of dirt eating exchanges between The Slam mer and your Warrior,the matchis calleda draw.Your Warrior gains nothing butthe knowledge that they lasted longer than most and has lost nothing morethan afew teeth... +6 With all the focused fury of a hungry Half ling in apie shop your Warrior meets The Slam merheadon.After several minutes of intense combat your Warrior takes the measure of his foe and slams the Norseman to the ground!Standingtriumphantamidstacheeringcrowd, your Warrior dusts off their clothing before marching off with 5o0 moregold pieces in their pocket.

event.name: Uneventful Day
event.rules: Uneventful day. No special effects.

event.name: Robbery
event.rules: Your Warrior bumps into a thief who hasjust robbed the jeweller's store.Roll1D6 on the table below: 1 The culprit escapes as the shop owner emerges and accuses your Warrior of the theft!Heim mediate ly summons the Watch who throw your Warrior in jail for 1D3 days and fine them 1D6x100 gold! 2-3 The robber throws some jewels at your Warrior,yelling "Thief!Thief!" and then escapes down a dark alley. Townsfolk come running out of the surroundings buildings and surround your Warrior,screamingforthe Watch.Afterrepeated pleasofinnocence,yourWarrior isfined 1D6x50 gold for failing to stop the crime. 4-5 Your Warrior instinctively knocks the culprit down, forcing him to drop allofhisloot.Theynimblethief manages to regain his feet and climb over a walland outofsight.Alocalwho witnessed everything corroborates your story to the shopkeeper and the Watch whenthey arrive.The owner rewards your Warrior with 1D6x25 gold for saving his merchandise. 6 YourWarriorgrabsthethief,slamminghimto thefloor and sitson him until the Watch arrive.Theshopkeeper lauds your Warrior as a hero and rewards them with 1D6x50gold!Inaddition,theyare given abronze pendant with an ancient rune of fortune inscribed upon it.Eachtimeyour Warrior visits a Settlement the pendantallowsyoutore-rolloneSettlementEvent.You m ust accept the result of the second roll. 20

event.name: Troubadour
event.rules: A colourfully-dressedbardnoticesyourWarriorastheypassby. With an exasperated laugh he follows your Warrior,loudly s exploits.Roll 1D6: 1-2 The raucous tune is an insulting account of all of your Warrior'sfollies;from their missteps in combatto spilling their drink atthetavern.The bard also sings of the unfortunate encounter with the miller's daughter down by the river which angers some of her close male relatives.They waitfor your Warrior around a corner, throw a sack over their head and beat them severely. Your Warriorsuffers-1 Strength forthe next Adventure. 3-4 The ballad is all about your Warrior's exploits but noone seemsto even notice or care. 5-6 Thebard embellishes all of your Warrior's good deeds and heroics to such degree that even your Warrior is impressed with themselves.Alarge group quickly gathers and gives three cheers!Your Warrior's spirit is bolstered and gains +1Strengthforthenext Adventure.

event.name: Tax Collector
event.rules: As your Warrior strolls about the civic district they are approached by a city employee who demands payment for the new ' Adventurer's Tax ' which amountsto10goldperTreasure item that they arecarrying.Your Warrior must either pay orthe local Watch will remove them from the City im m ediately (see

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Con Artist
event.rules: Mungo and his Amazing Monkeys are performing in the street and your Warrior laughs heartily as the creatures leap on to their back and scurry around theirlegs.Flipping a gold coin to the trainer your Warrior leaves the area.Aboutanhourlateryour Warrior realizes that they are missing one item of Treasure (determinedatrandom)!

event.name: Old Friend
event.rules: Someone across the street shouts out your Warrior'sname.Itis along lost friend and fellow Warrior who insists on taking your Warriortoeveryinnandalehouseheknowsin thevicinity. Your Warrior must roll on the Alehouse Events Table (ifusing theNew AlehouseEventsTable,the SophisticationValueisa2) 1D6 times,deducting a cumulative -1 from the score each successivevisit,astheybecome moreand more inebriated. At the end of the merry-making, your Warrior goes their separate way, leaving their old friend unconscious in a wharf sidebar of ill repute.

event.name: Good Deed
event.rules: your Warriorgoesoutonthetown,taking 1D6x20 gold with them.As they cross the street,they spotan old cronein an upstairs window shouting that robbers have broken into her attic room and are stealing her few possessions.Happy to assist the helpless,your Warrior bounds up the rickety stairsto herroom three at atime.As they enter herdimlylitroom,a blackbagisplacedovertheirhead,swiftlyfollowedbyaheavy ironbar.Theyawakenin thegutterseveralhourslater,nursing a sore head.Not surprisingly,the gold they wer carrying is all gone.

event.name: Beggars
event.rules: Your Warriorisaccostedbybeggarsand,overwhelmed by the sadness of theirplight,gives eachof the2D6+2 pitiful wretches 5 gold. If your Warrior cannot pay they are a beggar themselves,andyou mustroll1D6 on the following table: Your Warrior is thro wn outof the Settlem ent-see City 1 Event11. 2-6 YourWarriorgains1D6x15goldbybegging.

event.name: Relative
event.rules: An old woman bumps into your Warrior on the city street.She explodes in excitement at the sight of their face and claims they areherdistantrelative.Beforetheycansaya word,theexcited lady drags your Warrior home with her where they are welcomed by allthe family members and fed to their heart's content.Your Warrior graciouslyacceptstheirhospitality,which includesapackageoffoodupontheirdeparture. Your Warrior does not have to pay living expenses for this Sheet.

event.name: Criminal
event.rules: Your Warrior is minding theirown business when a criminal in custody of the Watchpassesby.Theprisonergivesanodanda smileto your Warrior and the captain of the guards notices. Roll1D6 on the table below: 1-3 Youarearrestedforcomplicity.Your Warrior spends thenext1D3 days in the local jail during which they are interrogated by the authorities. They are finally released with afine of1D6x5 gold for loitering. 4-6 One of the guards stops your Warrior for questioning as theother guardslookon.Thecriminaltakesthe opportunitytotryandbreakfree.Rollanother1D6. 1-3 The criminal manages to eludethe authorities and they are convinced that you werepurposelytryingtodistracttheguards. Youspendthenext1D3daysinjailuntil you're finally released due to lack of evidence. 4-6 As the prisoner makes a break forit,your Warrior frees him selffrom the bewildered guard,leapsoveravegetablestand,and apprehendsthecriminal.Dragginghim back to the Watch commander your Warrior collects1D6x25goldasareward.The prisoner glares at your Warrior as they are marched away.

event.name: Parade
event.rules: Alarge procession of citizens is marching down the avenue encouragingallonlookerstojoin them.Feelingin ajovialmood, your Warriordecidestojoin theparade.Roll1D6 on the table below: 1-2 The procession is a political activist group that is protestingthelocalauthorities.Allof the demonstrators,including your Warrior,are arrested by the Watch.Theyspendthenext1D3daysinjailand mustpay 1D6x20 goldinfines. 3-4 The procession is a victory march celebrating the most recentdefeatofOrcs& Goblinsin thearea.Thegroup marches to the city centre where a decorated commandergivesatriumphantspeech.YourWarrior mayadd+1to theirWillpowerforthenextAdventure! 5-6 The citizens are worshippers of the new temple whoare trying to recruit members. The procession makesits way to the temple where all of the worshippers, including your Warrior,are blessed by the head priest. Your Warrior is +1Luck for the next Adventure!

event.name: Accident
event.rules: As your Warrior helps an old crone to cross the busy main street,a beer wagon unexpectedly crashes into them. She carefully peels them offthe heavy,iron-shed wheel,andtakes them to the infirmary to be patched up.Your Warrior cannot buy anything or visit any Special Locations for 1D6 days while

event.name: City Watch
event.rules: Your Warrior is accosted by the City Watch who has been warned that a Warrior bearing powerful and destructive magic items is on the prowl. Your Warrior mustroll1D6foreachitem of Treasure they arecarrying.Onascore 1 the Watch confiscate and it must be discarded im mediately.

event.name: Street Urchin
event.rules: Your Warrior is approached by a street urchin who offers to guard their mountfor 10 gold pieces a day.If your Warrior pays the urchin, you may rolltwo dice instead of one when determining if your Warrior's horse has been stolen and may choose the higher one. If your Warrior does not have that amount or decides not to pay the urchin,then treat this as an Uneventful Day.

event.name: Salesman
event.rules: An extremely persuasive salesman is making a pitch toalarge crowd.Roll1D6andaddyourWarrior's Willpower: 2-4 YourWarriorpays1D6x1o0 gold for a pair of boots yourfeetsmell..." 5-7 Your Warrior purchases a one year subscription to Adventurer's Monthlyfor1D6x50gold.This manuscript contains everything one needs to know about slaying monsters and keeping their gear in pristine condition! The manuscript willbe deliveredevery3o days directly toyour Warrior's doorstep! 8+ Your Warrior resists the great temptation to buy Super Belt,the all purpose survival kit that will hold the most important items one should carry in the wild including coins,abeermug,arope,rations,aknife,alargesack and aQua rg horn.

event.name: Duel
event.rules: Your Warrior is challenged to a duelby a professional duelist whom they insulted in atavern.The duelis scheduled to take place atdawn tomorrow. Your Warrior can either leave town im mediate ly or take up the challenge. If your Warrior takes part in the duel,roll 1D6 on the following table: 1 With asingle, well placed sword thrust your Warrior's heartisspearedand theyfallto theground,quitedead. 2-4 Thetwo duelistsfightforhourafterhour,eachinflicting manylightwoundsontheother.Eventuallythe sun goesdownand thefightisdeclaredadraw.The duelist congratulates your Warrior on their swordsman ship, and offers them a fines upper and the best wine at the most expensive hotel in the Settlement. 5-6 After a few minutes,your Warrior realizes that they have the better of the duellistanddespatcheshim.On his body they find jewels worth2D6x50 gold anda single item of Dungeon Room Treasure.

event.name: An Adventure!
event.rules: See The Ultimate Adventure Book to determine what exploit the Warriors might participate in next!

event.name: Riot
event.rules: The lowly inhabitants outside the Settlement are fed up with their impoverished situation.A small gathering has erupted into a dangerous riot pitting the peasants against the local Watch. Your Warrior chooses to help restrain the peasants and must roll2D6onthetable below: 2 An angry horde of peasants overpowers your Warrior and theybeatthemintoabloodypulp.Theyaredead and must be removed from game. 3-5 Your Warrior is overrun by vagabonds who beat them senseless.They spendthenext1D6daysin the infirmary and mustpay1D6x50goldfortreatment. 6-9 Your Warrior helps the Watch beat back the miscreants and is paid 1D6x25 gold in wages. 10+ Your Warrior helps crush the riot quickly and efficiently. A high official is extremely impressed and pleased by your Warrior'sactions.Hepaysthem 1D6x50 goldand gives them oneitem of Dungeon Room Treasure!

event.name: Trader
event.rules: A shrewd trader convinces your Warrior to swap anitem of Treasure(determinedatrandom)foroneofhis. Make one roll onthe Dungeon Room Treasure Tableto see whatitem the traderis giving youin exchange.Ifthe gold value of the trader's item is m ore than the gold value of the Warrior's item,the Traderisnothappy.Rollanother1D6 andif the result is a 1, the Trader declares that you were being deceptive in the trade and summons the Watch who declare that both items must be returned to their original owners.

event.name: Reward
event.rules: Your Warrior sees a poster warning that a well known murderer isreputedtobein thearea,andoffersarewardforhiscapture. The criminal in question is described as very tall, wearing a black cloak and hat,and having an Erengrad accent. Upon reading this, your Warrior realises that the description exactly fits a character they bumped into earlier,and rushing back, manages to apprehend the villain just as he dragsa gagged merchant into a dark alley.Roll1D6 on the following table: 1 The murdererlaughs,slitsthemerchant'sthroat, throws the knife at your Warrior's feet andflees, shouting"Help,murder! Soonyour Warrior is

event.name: Assassin
event.rules: (and1D6x50)goldcoinspersuadethem that they are innocent. Your Warrior savesthe m erchant'slife,for which he 2-5 gives them 20gold,butthemurdererslipsawayinto thegloom. Your Warrior frees the merchant and captures the 6 murderer,forwhichtheyareproclaimedaheroand given1o0 goldreward. ASSASSIN As your Warrior passes byan alleyway they catch sightofa figure lurking in the darkness. They instinctively draw their weaponjustasthefigurepouncesonthem!Roll1D6 andadd your Warrior's Initiative: 2-4 Your Warrior is severely wounded!Roll1D6 andifthe resultisa1,yourWarriorcannotbesavedand perishes.OnanyotherscoreyourWarriorisfoundby a passerby who rushes them to the infirmary where they are treated for their grievous wounds.They spendthenext1D6daysinbedand mustdeduct1 Wound permanently from their Starting Woundsscore. Your Warrior suffers a serious wound as they fend off 5-7 theattacker,whoquicklydisappearsdownthe alleyway.Your Warriormustspendthenext1D3days in theinfirmary,paying 1D6x20 gold for treatm ent as well as Living Expenses. 8-10 Your Warrior defends himself against the assailant but the attacker manages to escape. 11+ Your Warrior deals a prompt and fatal blow to the villain.Upon searching the Assassin's body your Warrior finds oneItem of Dungeon Room Treasure on hisperson,butnoclueastowhohewasorwhyhe was trying to killyour Warrior.
